question_text: A water wave has a wavelength of 2 metres and a frequency of 5 Hz. Calculate the wave speed using the equation: wave speed = frequency × wavelength.
explanation: To find wave speed, multiply frequency by wavelength using the equation: wave speed = frequency × wavelength. Here, 5 Hz × 2 m = 10 m/s. This tells us the wave travels 10 metres in one second.
